mechanism: Dihydropyridine CCBs cause preferential arteriolar vasodilation without matching venodilation → increased capillary hydrostatic pressure → fluid transudation into interstitial space. Dose-dependent: ~5% at 5mg, ~30% at 10mg amlodipine. Not true fluid retention — diuretics are ineffective.

contextualModifiers.effect: dose-dependent increase in pedal oedema incidence

sourceSnippet: StatPearls CCBs; BMJ Best Practice Hypertension
